sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Pendo connector** integrates with the Pendo product analytics platform via the Pendo Aggregation API. It enables Shielva to sync guides, features, pages, accounts, and visitors from your Pendo subscription into the knowledge base.

This connector uses the **x-pendo-integration-key** header for authentication on every request.

**Key capabilities:**
- Validate credentials via `GET /api/v1/app` on install and health check
- Sync in-app guides and tagged features for all apps
- Direct access to pages, accounts, and visitors via the aggregation pipeline
- Stable document IDs via `SHA-256(type:id)[:16]` for deduplication across syncs
- Automatic retry with exponential back-off and circuit breaker protection

children.title: Connector Type
children.content: `pendo` — registered in ACP as **Pendo**.

children.title: Provider
children.content: Pendo (Pendo Aggregation API — `https://app.pendo.io/api/v1/...`)

children.title: Supported Operations
children.content:
| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `install()` | Validates integration_key via GET /api/v1/app; returns HEALTHY/CONNECTED or OFFLINE with reason |
| `health_check()` | Same call; returns app count in message |
| `sync()` | Fetches guides + features for all apps; normalizes to ConnectorDocuments |
| `list_apps()` | GET /api/v1/app — all Pendo applications |
| `list_guides(app_id)` | GET /api/v1/guide — in-app guides for an app |
| `list_features(app_id)` | GET /api/v1/feature — tagged features for an app |
| `list_pages(app_id)` | GET /api/v1/page — pages for an app |
| `list_accounts()` | POST /api/v1/aggregation — account pipeline |
| `list_visitors()` | POST /api/v1/aggregation — visitor pipeline |

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content: The connector authenticates using the **x-pendo-integration-key** header on every request. The integration key is sent as a custom header — not as HTTP Basic Auth.

children.title: Auth Type
children.content: `api_key` — Integration Key found in Pendo → Settings → Integrations → Integration Keys.

children.title: Install Fields
children.content:
| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| Integration Key | `integration_key` | password | Yes | Pendo Integration Key for the subscription |

children.title: Credential Validation
children.content: The connector validates the key by calling `GET /api/v1/app`. A 401 or 403 response returns `INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. A network error returns `FAILED`. Success returns `HEALTHY/CONNECTED` with the app count.

children.title: Credential Security
children.content: Integration Keys do not expire unless manually revoked in Pendo. Shielva stores the key AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest via the vault. The key never appears in log output.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content: All configuration is stored in the connector's `config` dict (encrypted at rest by Shielva vault).

children.title: Config Keys
children.content:
| Key | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `integration_key` | Pendo Integration Key |

children.title: Request Headers
children.content:
Every request includes:
```
x-pendo-integration-key: <integration_key>
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json
```

children.title: Aggregation API

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All methods are implemented in `connector.py` and delegate HTTP calls to `PendoHTTPClient` in `client/http_client.py`.

children.title: install()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def install(self) -> InstallResult`

Validates the integration key and establishes the connector.

**Steps:**
1. Check `integration_key` is non-empty → MISSING_CREDENTIALS if not.
2. Call `GET /api/v1/app` via `with_retry`.
3. On success, create a fresh http_client for subsequent use.

**Returns:**
- `InstallResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, message='Connected to Pendo')`
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` — missing key
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` — 401/403
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=FAILED)` — network/server error

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def health_check(self) -> HealthCheckResult`

Calls `GET /api/v1/app` and returns health with app count in the message.

**Returns:**
- `HealthCheckResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, message='Connected to Pendo (2 app(s))')`
- `HealthCheckResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` on 401/403
- `HealthCheckResult(health=DEGRADED, auth_status=FAILED)` on network error (circuit breaker not open)
- `HealthCheckResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=FAILED)` on network error (circuit breaker open)

children.title: sync()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def sync(self, full=False, since=None, kb_id='') -> SyncResult`

Full sync across all Pendo apps:
1. Fetch all apps via `GET /api/v1/app`.
2. For each app: fetch guides (`GET /api/v1/guide`) and features (`GET /api/v1/feature`).
3. Normalize each guide/feature to a ConnectorDocument.
4. Optionally ingest into the knowledge base when `kb_id` is provided.

Per-app failures are non-fatal — only a total app-fetch failure causes FAILED status.

**Returns:** `SyncResult` with documents_found, documents_synced, documents_failed.
Status: `COMPLETED` when no failures; `PARTIAL` when some fail; `FAILED` if apps cannot be fetched.

children.title: list_apps()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_apps(self) -> list[dict]`

Fetches all Pendo applications in the subscription via `GET /api/v1/app`.

**Returns:** List of app dicts (id, name, displayName, etc.).

children.title: list_guides()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_guides(self, app_id: str = '') -> list[dict]`

Fetches in-app guides for a given app via `GET /api/v1/guide`.

**Returns:** List of guide dicts (id, name, state, kind, lastUpdatedAt, etc.).

children.title: list_features()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_features(self, app_id: str = '') -> list[dict]`

Fetches tagged features for a given app via `GET /api/v1/feature`.

**Returns:** List of feature dicts (id, name, kind, color, etc.).

children.title: list_pages()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_pages(self, app_id: str = '') -> list[dict]`

Fetches pages for a given app via `GET /api/v1/page`.

**Returns:** List of page dicts (id, name, kind, etc.).

children.title: list_accounts()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_accounts(self, per_page: int = 100, page_number: int = 0) -> dict`

Fetches accounts via the Pendo Aggregation API (`POST /api/v1/aggregation`).

**Returns:** Aggregation response dict with `results` list.

children.title: list_visitors()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_visitors(self, per_page: int = 100) -> dict`

Fetches visitors via the Pendo Aggregation API (`POST /api/v1/aggregation`).

**Returns:** Aggregation response dict with `results` list.

sections.title: Data Model
sections.content: Normalized documents produced by normalizer functions in `helpers/utils.py`.

children.title: Guide ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "source_id": "<sha256('guide:' + guide_id)[:16]>",
  "title": "Pendo guide: Onboarding Tour",
  "content": "Guide ID: guide_aaa\nName: Onboarding Tour\nState: public\nKind: lightbox",
  "source_url": "https://app.pendo.io/guides",
  "metadata": {
    "type": "guide",
    "guide_id": "guide_aaa",
    "name": "Onboarding Tour",
    "state": "public",
    "kind": "lightbox"
  }
}
```

children.title: Feature ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "source_id": "<sha256('feature:' + feature_id)[:16]>",
  "title": "Pendo feature: Export Button",
  "content": "Feature ID: feat_111\nName: Export Button\nKind: click",
  "source_url": "https://app.pendo.io/features",
  "metadata": {
    "type": "feature",
    "feature_id": "feat_111",
    "name": "Export Button",
    "kind": "click"
  }
}
```

children.title: Document ID Stability
children.content:
All documents use `SHA-256(type + ':' + resource_id)[:16]` as the stable `source_id`. This is deterministic across syncs — the same Pendo resource always produces the same Shielva document ID, enabling upsert deduplication without storing seen-ID lists.

| Resource | ID formula |
|----------|------------|
| Guide | `SHA-256('guide:' + guide_id)[:16]` |
| Feature | `SHA-256('feature:' + feature_id)[:16]` |
| Page | `SHA-256('page:' + page_id)[:16]` |
| Account | `SHA-256('account:' + accountId)[:16]` |

sections.title: Exception Hierarchy
sections.content:
All exceptions inherit from `PendoError` (base) in `exceptions.py`:

```
PendoError (base)
├── PendoAuthError            — 401, 403 responses
├── PendoNetworkError         — connectivity failures, timeouts
├── PendoRateLimitError       — 429 Too Many Requests (has retry_after attr)
├── PendoNotFoundError        — 404 Not Found
└── PendoServerError          — 5xx responses
```

Callers can catch the base `PendoError` or specific subclasses for finer control.

children.title: Common Errors
children.content:
| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| `integration_key is required` | No key provided | Enter the Integration Key in the install form |
| `401 Unauthorized` | Invalid or revoked key | Generate a new Integration Key in Pendo → Settings → Integrations |
| `403 Forbidden` | Insufficient subscription access | Verify your Pendo plan includes API access |
| `429 Too Many Requests` | Rate limit exceeded | Connector retries automatically with exponential back-off |
| No data synced | No apps or no guides/features | Verify your Pendo subscription has at least one app with data |

children.title: Testing
children.content:
Run the full test suite (all mocked — no live credentials needed):

```bash
cd /Users/vivekvarshavaishvik/Documents/client_dir/pendo_connector
source "/Volumes/V3-SSD/Shielva Project Dirs/.venv/bin/activate"
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

All tests must pass before registering the connector.
